Micromanipulation and Microassembly We have developed a set of dextrous micromanipulation primitives for reorienting and regrasping rectangular parts. The parts can be combined to build 3 dimensional microstructures. Some examples from Shimada e

al microstructures. Some examples from Shimada et al [2000] are shown here. ``Pivot'' grasp using fixture to generate moment on 75 by 100 by 400 micron part, rotating part. Movie of automated assembly sequence (MPEG 1.48 MB) . (MPEG 16 MB) . Thi

mbly sequence (MPEG 1.48 MB) . (MPEG 16 MB) . This operation is completely open-loop, resulting in accumulated alignment errors. Stacked microparts, bonded with UV cure epoxy. 3 large base parts are 400 by 230 by 75 microns thick, top part is 80 b

e 400 by 230 by 75 microns thick, top part is 80 by 80 by 430 microns. Eiji Shimada's Microassembly page Papers on Microassembly, etc. (see also Desktop Rapid Prototyping Millirobots Publications and Micromechanical Flying Insect Publications ) Appar
